prompt: A catalog price change scheduled exactly when the prior price expires either fails to publish or briefly produces two prices for one lookup instant, depending on which environment created the history. Prices well inside an effective period resolve correctly. Repair the write-time and read-time temporal contract together so contiguous history is legal, overlap remains impossible, and every boundary instant resolves unambiguously.

repo_files.db/resolved_prices.sql:
CREATE VIEW resolved_prices AS
SELECT request.request_id, price.sku, price.amount_cents
FROM price_requests AS request
JOIN prices AS price
  ON price.sku = request.sku
 AND price.valid_from <= request.at_time
 AND (price.valid_to IS NULL OR request.at_time <= price.valid_to);
repo_files.db/catalog_seed.sql:
INSERT INTO prices(sku, amount_cents, valid_from, valid_to)
VALUES ('sample', 100, '2026-01-01T00:00:00Z', NULL);
